review_reason: The research identifies a core mathematical obstruction: any duration-sensitive non-trivial task_rel requires quotientSucc/quotientPred to be mutual inverses, which requires the semantically invalid G(phi)->H(phi). Two options presented: (A) direction-insensitive CanonicalR-based task_rel (non-trivial but ignores duration d), or (B) mark implementation BLOCKED pending resolution of the inverse property. User decision needed on which option to pursue.
summary: Research-019 identifies the core obstruction to constructing D from syntax: any compositionality-satisfying task_rel on BidirectionalQuotient requires quotientSucc_pred_inverse (blocked by invalid G(phi)->H(phi)). Recommends D=Int with CanonicalR-based task_rel. Finds orderIsoIntOfLinearSuccPredArch in Mathlib but its prerequisites (SuccOrder) are blocked by coverness.

artifacts.summary: Comprehensive analysis of D construction strategies. Key findings: (1) No intrinsic AddCommGroup on BidirectionalQuotient, (2) SuccOrder blocked by coverness, (3) AddTorsor blocked by inverse property, (4) Direction-insensitive CanonicalR task_rel is strongest achievable non-trivial option

partial_progress.details: Research-019 report written (569 lines). Core finding: duration-sensitive non-trivial task_rel is mathematically blocked. Recommends direction-insensitive CanonicalR-based task_rel with D=Int.
